title: SportsPredictionSystem - Quick Start Pamphlet
overview: A compact instruction pamphlet to onboard users: what the system does, main commands, and how to read outputs.

sections.heading: What it does
sections.body: Generates match predictions (Phase 2 Lite), collects match results, computes accuracy, and runs automated optimization experiments. It can fetch finished match results, create historical datasets, run parameter sweeps, and optionally notify and generate reports.

items.cmd: python generate_fast_reports.py generate 1 matches for la-liga
items.desc: Generate 1 match report for La Liga.

items.cmd: python generate_fast_reports.py generate 6 matches for all --delay 60
items.desc: Generate 6 matches per league with 60s delay between match runs to avoid API rate limits.
Collect predictions from reports into historical staging for La Liga.
Fetch finished match results from APIs and update historical file for La Liga.
Fetch all leagues, auto-run optimizer when ≥50 completed matches, send email and generate report.
Run backtest / optimization (simulated if no history).
Run the daily local pipeline: fetch, backfill provider IDs, prune + regenerate reports (recommended local scheduler).
Wait between matches when generating multiple reports to avoid API limits.
Skip injury API calls to reduce rate-limit risk and speed up runs.
Fetch finished match results from configured APIs for the provided --league.
Fetch finished match results for all supported leagues.
If league completed matches ≥ N, run full optimization automatically.
Send notification email summary after fetch (requires SMTP env vars).
Generate a human-readable Markdown summary report in reports/historical/.

sections.heading: Where to look for results
sections.body: Reports saved under reports/leagues/<league>/matches/<match_folder>/ (prediction.json and summary), and summary reports at reports/historical/

sections.heading: How matching works (simple)
sections.body: To link API results to predictions we match date + home/away team names using a canonical team-name map (config/team_name_map.yaml). If names differ slightly a fuzzy match (small string distance) is used as a fallback.

items.fix: Use --delay between matches or --no-injuries, and set throttle values in config/settings.yaml (api.football-data.org: 6.0).
Ensure API keys (FOOTBALL_DATA_API_KEY or API_FOOTBALL_KEY) are set and that prediction reports exist for the matches.
Set SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASS, EMAIL_TO).

sections.heading: Quick Onboarding Checklist
items: 2. Add API keys to .env or environment: FOOTBALL_DATA_API_KEY, API_FOOTBALL_KEY
3. Run sample report: python generate_fast_reports.py generate 1 matches for la-liga
4. Collect and fetch results: python scripts/collect_historical_results.py --fetch --league la-liga
5. Check reports at reports/leagues and summary at reports/historical/

items.cmd: schtasks /Create /SC DAILY /TN "SportsPredictionSystem - Daily Fetch" /TR "powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File "C:\Path\To\STATS\scripts\run_daily_fetch.ps1"" /ST 03:00 /RL LIMITED
items.desc: Schedule the script to run daily at 03:00 (replace path and time as needed).
Run in debug mode to capture verbose logs in logs/daily/.
Install the Windows scheduled task to run the daily pipeline (run this locally once).

sections.heading: Support & Contacts
sections.body: Add a contact or README note here for the team responsible for the system and where to send issues (e.g., GitHub issues or internal tracker).
